mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in < 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0% signals weakening labor market conditions, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in < 30% of triggered instances or t-test p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate supply-side shocks and inflationary pressures, causing market uncertainty and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances with both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL declining or rising respectively.

mechanism_narrative: A 4-quarter decline in GDP (GDPC1) signals weakening demand, prompting central banks to stimulate the economy, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in < 35% of instances despite GDPC1 decline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in CPIAUCSL indicates deflationary pressures, prompting central banks to lower rates, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances with CPIAUCSL decline.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1600 signals weak demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in < 25% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payrolls (PAYEMS) and decreases in unemployment (UNRATE) indicate a strong labor market, boosting consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances with both PAYEMS up and UNRATE down.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle, increasing borrowing costs and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in < 30% of triggered instances or t-test p > 0.10.
